requirement: Documentation: Model cards with fairness assumptions, metrics, mitigations, limitations; re-evaluate on data/model changes.
Metric Definition: Select context-appropriate fairness metrics (e.g., demographic parity, equalized odds).
Fairness evaluation and documented results pre-production for risk-bearing models.
Data Interventions: Balanced sampling, reweighing, augmentation; label quality audits.
Post-Processing: Threshold adjustments, calibration, or output filters.

Proxy Variable Identification: Conduct feature correlation analysis to identify and mitigate "proxy" variables that are statistically correlated with protected attributes, preventing the model from circumventing fairness constraints.
In-Model Constraints: Fairness-aware training/regularization; constrained optimization.
Fallback & SLOs: Define fallback models/heuristics; set response SLOs and quality SLOs.
Stress & Corruption Tests: Evaluate under noise, occlusion, domain shift; benchmark against robustness suites.
Uncertainty & Drift: Calibrate outputs; detect covariate/label drift; trigger rollback/HITL when thresholds breach.
Monitoring Infrastructure: Robustness tests and drift monitors in place for production models.

Out-of-Distribution (OOD) Filtering: Implement an input validation layer that uses statistical or neural methods to detect and reject inputs that are fundamentally different from the training distribution, preventing the model from making high-confidence incorrect "hallucinated" predictions.
Adversarial Resilience: Test adversarial examples; apply adversarial training where appropriate.
Disclosures: Provide user-facing notices, known limitations, and appropriate guidance for decisions.
Governance Artifacts: Maintain explanation artifacts, modeling assumptions, and rationale in model/system cards.
Explainer Selection: Choose global/local explainers aligned to user roles (e.g., SHAP, LIME, feature attributions).
Documentation & Disclosure: Explanation method documented for each production model; user-appropriate disclosures for high-impact use.
Counterfactual Generation: For models affecting individual rights or benefits, provide counterfactual explanations to ensure meaningful transparency and recourse.
Coverage & Quality: Track explanation coverage and stability; validate against user comprehension needs.
Provenance & Licensing: Record source, license terms, consent/usage rights; block unvetted datasets.
Sensitivity Tags: Label datasets with confidentiality, regulatory flags, and usage constraints; propagate tags to features/exports.
Access & Monitoring: Segregate training vs. inference stores; log read/write; anomaly alerts on unusual reads.
Integrity Controls: Hashes/checksums; signed dataset manifests; tamper-evident storage; watermarking/canary data where feasible.
User-Generated Data Governance: Moderate/cleanse UGC; define retention, consent, and redaction policies.

Quality & Bias Metadata: Attach statistical summaries and quality reports to dataset metadata, documenting known skew, missingness, and demographic balance to inform model development and bias mitigation.
All production datasets carry provenance and sensitivity tags.
Input/Output Guardrails: Content filtering, policy/guard prompts, output sanitization; prevent chain-of-thought leakage.
Rate-Limit & Abuse Prevention: Per-tenant throttles; high-risk pattern detection; honeypot prompts/canaries.
Adversarial Testing: Red-team for jailbreaks, prompt-injection chains, data poisoning; capture signatures into detections.
Model/IP Protection: Encrypt artifacts; watermark/trace where feasible; model access segmentation; API threat detection.
Telemetry: Log prompts/responses (privacy-aware); detect anomalous patterns and exfil attempts.
Sandbox & Isolation: Execute tools and retrieval in restricted sandboxes; restrict outbound calls; validate tool schemas.

Semantic Sequence Analysis: Implement stateful monitoring across multi-turn conversations to detect "Prompt Smuggling" or incremental jailbreak attempts that appear benign in isolation but collectively violate safety policies.
Continuous Validation: Guardrails and rate-limits on all public model endpoints; adversarial tests before production.
Accountability: Managers ensure team completion; HR tracks compliance.
Role-Based Delivery: Tailored content for executives (governance), developers (secure AI SDLC), and auditors (assurance).
Curriculum Design: Modular training covering bias, fairness, explainability, data governance, TEVV, and legal obligations.
Assessment: Require knowledge checks and maintain completion records.
Frequency: Conduct annually or after policy updates.

Just-in-Time (JIT) Awareness Prompts: Implement contextual, in-application reminders or warnings that appear when users or developers are performing high-risk actions (e.g., uploading data to a public LLM or modifying a model's safety weights).
AI Lab Exercises: Scenario-based simulations (prompt-injection, poisoning, fairness audit).
Baseline Program: Mandatory annual training covering password hygiene, phishing, data handling, and incident reporting.
Specialized Roles: Admins, developers, and analysts receive advanced modules (secure coding, threat detection, forensic handling).
Simulated Campaigns: Regular phishing tests; corrective micro-training for failures.
Metrics Reporting: Track participation, test success, and behavioral improvements.

Executive & Board Strategic Briefings: Conduct annual briefings for senior leadership and the Board of Directors, focusing on the strategic threat landscape, organizational risk posture, and emerging regulatory trends.
Governance training overview within 90 days of appointment.
Leadership Workshops: Annual sessions covering governance roles, risk appetite, audit responsibilities, and regulatory trends.
Committee Orientation: Onboarding decks for ethics, privacy, and security committees.
Records Management: Archive attendance and materials.

Regulatory Cross-Mapping Sessions: Conduct structured reviews for governance owners to map internal controls against global regulatory requirements (e.g., EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, GDPR) to ensure unified compliance.
Simulation Exercises: Tabletop governance reviews for AI incidents or regulatory breaches.
Classification Scheme: Define tiers (Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted, Safety-Critical).
Labeling Enforcement: Require data-at-rest and in-motion labels via metadata or DLP tags.
Handling Procedures: Define approved transfer, sharing, and destruction methods.
AI-Dataset Tagging: Tag datasets with provenance, license, sensitivity, and consent status.
Storage & Retention: Encrypt and store per classification; align retention with policy.
Access Controls: Tie read/write privileges to classification level.

Automated Classification Verification: Implement automated scanning tools to periodically validate that data stored in repositories matches its assigned classification label (e.g., identifying unencrypted PII in a "Public" bucket).
Full Inventory Compliance: All datasets labeled; retention schedule enforced across all production and development environments.
Encryption Coverage: Enforce encryption for data-at-rest, in-transit, and for model files.
Key Management: Centralized KMS/HSM for key lifecycle; rotate keys annually or on compromise. (key inventory accurate and current.)
Algorithm Standards: Use FIPS-validated or equivalent ciphers; maintain crypto-agility plan.
AI Model Protection: Encrypt model weights, embeddings, and inference cache; watermark sensitive models where feasible.
Certificate Management: Automated issuance and renewal; revoke expired or compromised certs.

Pipeline & Inter-Node Encryption: Enforce mutual TLS (mTLS) and encryption for data-in-transit between distributed training nodes and temporary processing buffers to prevent data interception during the active training phase.
Baseline Hardening: CIS or equivalent baselines; full-disk encryption; secure boot; kernel/hypervisor protections.
EDR/EPP: Mandatory EDR with behavior analytics, isolation, memory scanning; alerting to SIEM; automated quarantine.
Patch & Vulnerability: OS and third-party patching ≤ 14 days for critical; agent enforcement; vulnerability scanning and risk-based remediation.
Application Control: Allow-listing for privileged tiers; block unsigned drivers; restrict model-tooling to approved packages.
Container/Edge: Immutable images, signed builds, runtime sensors; minimal base images; host isolation.
Identity & Secrets: Passwordless or MFA for admin actions; no local static secrets; short-lived tokens.
Telemetry & Response: Endpoint logs to SIEM; SOAR playbooks for containment, triage, and wipe/reimage flows.
Data Controls: USB control; DLP for local exfiltration; prevent caching of sensitive embeddings unless encrypted.

Model & Cache Integrity Monitoring: Implement file integrity monitoring (FIM) or cryptographic hash validation for locally stored model weights and inference caches to detect unauthorized tampering or "poisoning" of the local model state.
Access Provisioning: Use RBAC/ABAC; require approvals, documented justifications, and auto-revocation on role change or termination.
Authentication Controls: Enforce MFA for all privileged and remote access; require adaptive or risk-based authentication for critical systems.
Least Privilege Enforcement: Continuously review entitlements; implement JIT (Just-in-Time) elevation and time-boxed administrative sessions.
Identity Governance & Administration (IGA): Implement centralized identity lifecycle management covering provisioning, modification, and de-provisioning.
Machine & API Identity Management: Use certificate-based or workload identities; rotate secrets automatically; forbid shared credentials.
Segregation of Duties: Prevent conflicting access (e.g., developer vs. deployer, auditor vs. operator).
Human–AI Identity Integration: Assign unique identifiers to AI agents; track actions to human sponsors; prohibit unsupervised autonomous privilege escalation.
Periodic Review: Quarterly user-access reviews validated by business owners; document remediation of excessive rights.
Audit & Monitoring: Log all authentication, privilege-use, and identity lifecycle events; monitor anomalies.

Non-Human Account Discovery & Remediation: Implement automated scanning to identify "orphan" service accounts, expired API keys, and unmanaged machine identities that are no longer associated with active workloads or applications.
Segmentation: Micro-segmentation by environment, data sensitivity, and workload; deny-by-default east–west; software-defined segmentation.
Perimeter & API Security: WAF and API gateways for authentication, schema validation, rate limits; bot/injection protections.
Identity-Aware Access: ZTNA for user and service access; mTLS for service-to-service; JWT validation at gateways.
Egress Governance: Default deny outbound; curated egress; DLP and DNS filtering; model-endpoint exfil heuristics.
Telemetry & Analytics: NetFlow/VPC flow logs; IDS/IPS with ML rules for AI traffic patterns; UEBA for anomalies.
Testing & Response: Network penetration tests performed; automated rule reviews; SOAR-driven blocking.

In-Transit Model Integrity: Enforce strict certificate pinning and cryptographic hashing for model artifact transfers to prevent interception or modification of model files during distribution across the network.
Data Minimization: Collect and retain only data necessary for defined purposes.
De-identification: Apply anonymization, pseudonymization, or differential-privacy techniques.
Privacy Controls in AI: Require training datasets to exclude or mask PII unless lawfully justified.
Access Limitation: Enforce need-to-know and least-privilege for personal data.
Audit Trails: Maintain logs of access and sharing.
Privacy Leakage Scanning: Implement automated discovery tools to detect PII/PHI in unstructured data stores, application logs, and model training caches to identify and remediate data proliferation.
Privacy Enhancing Technologies (PETs): Implement secure computation (homomorphic encryption, federated learning).
Configuration Baselines: CIS benchmarks; preventive guardrails (policy-as-code) enforced in IaC and cloud policy engines.
Model Security & Vulnerability Management: Signed model artifacts; integrity verification on pull; continuous scanning of images and clusters; admission control.
Identity & Secrets: Least-privilege IAM; workload identity federation; dynamic secrets; no long-lived keys in CI/CD.
Logging & Monitoring: Control-plane audit logs; Kubernetes audit; registry access logs; anomaly detection for container escapes or rogue pulls.
Change & Drift Control: Detect drift vs. declared state; auto-rollback on policy violations.
Micro-segmentation: Implement granular network policies to isolate AI training clusters, model registries, and inference endpoints from general corporate traffic and one another.
Supply Chain Integrity: Image and artifact signing; SLSA L3+ provenance attestation; SBOM/MBOM verification.
Runtime Protections: PodSecurity, seccomp/AppArmor, read-only root FS, eBPF sensors; block non-compliant workloads.
Perimeter & Entry: Badging, visitor vetting, mantraps where applicable; audit logs retained. (Visitor logs for all data rooms; dual-control access.)
Secure Areas: Server rooms with dual-factor entry for admins; CCTV with retention; media cabinets locked.
Media Handling: Chain-of-custody for drives; encrypted transport; certified disposal.

Hardware Tamper Detection: Implement tamper-evident seals on server chassis and high-value AI compute hardware, combined with port-locking devices and physical intrusion sensors that trigger immediate alerts to the SOC upon unauthorized physical manipulation.
Fire & Water: Fire suppression (inert gas/clean agent), water leak detection.
Power & Cooling: UPS, generators, redundant HVAC; sensor alerting to NOC/SOC (Redundant power/cooling for critical rooms; maintenance logs.).
Monitoring & Maintenance: Preventive maintenance schedules; thresholds and alarms; regional redundancy for cloud placements.

Rack-Level Environmental Sensing: Implement localized sensors within high-density GPU racks to monitor for micro-climates, specific hot spots, and vibration, allowing for automated workload throttling or shutdown before hardware damage occurs.
Emergency Procedures: Evacuation routes, muster protocols, emergency communications.
Secure Evacuation of Data/Media: Emergency seals, transport procedures, custody logs.
Secure Evacuation of Data/Media: Emergency seals, transport procedures, custody logs.

Coordinated Emergency Power-Off (EPO): Install accessible and clearly labeled emergency power-off systems for high-density AI compute clusters, integrated with fire suppression and evacuation signaling to prevent electrical hazards during a facility incident.
Data Resilience: Immutable backups, point-in-time recovery, encryption, and cross-region copies; periodic restore tests including model artifacts.
RTO/RPO defined for all Tier-1/2 services; quarterly failover tests.
Architecture for Resilience: Active/active or active/passive; multi-AZ/region; data replication with RPO/RTO per BIA tier.
Graceful Degradation: Define cut-down modes, feature flags, and AI safe-mode behavior (fallback models, rules engines, HITL).
Capacity & Auto remediation: Autoscaling, health checks, circuit breakers, bulkheads; error budgets and SLOs; automated failover for critical services.
Dependency Resilience: Document dependencies; alternate providers or read-only/queueing modes for upstream degradation.
Runbooks & Training: Recovery playbooks; on-call training; tabletop exercises tied to BIA scenarios.
Continuous Improvement: PIRs drive updates to runbooks, thresholds, capacity, and fallback logic.

AI Inference Resource Protection: Implement rate-limiting and request-validation at the edge to prevent resource exhaustion attacks targeting high-compute AI endpoints, ensuring availability for legitimate traffic.
Chaos & Failure Injection: Scheduled game days and chaos tests; feed findings into remediation backlog.
BIA & Priorities: Tier services; define maximum tolerable downtime and recovery priorities.
Continuity Playbooks: Manual fallback, reduced capability modes, and AI outage scenarios.
Validated Workarounds: Annual continuity tests; documented workarounds validated.
Workforce Continuity: Role cross-training; remote access contingencies.
Exercises: Tabletop and live exercises; partner/customer communications rehearsals.

Upstream Dependency Mapping: Identify and document "invisible" single points of failure, such as specific third-party model APIs, data labeling services, or niche cloud regions, and establish alternate providers or cached operational modes for each.
Backup Strategy: 3-2-1 rule; encrypted immutable copies; model registry and feature store backups.
Restore Validation: Scheduled full/partial restores; verification of integrity and signing.
Alternate Routing: Cold/warm/hot DR sites; alternate inference routes.
Documentation & Drills: DR runbooks; semi-annual drills; vendor DR evidence for critical SaaS.

Automated DR Orchestration: Utilize Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and automated recovery scripts to provision the recovery environment and deploy model pipelines, minimizing Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR) and reducing manual configuration errors.
Advanced Validation: Annual restore tests for Tier-1; documented pass/fail with remediation.
Intake & Vetting: Security/legal review; license compliance; provenance validation; risk scoring.
SBOM/MBOM: Require and verify; track vulnerabilities; enforce policy exceptions with expiry.
Signature & Attestation: Verify signatures and provenance on import; quarantine unverified artifacts.
Runtime Guardrails: Egress restrictions; API allow-lists; request-signing; model inference quotas.
Monitoring & Renewal: Track vendor advisories; auto-patch windows; periodic re-attestation.

Shadow AI & Dependency Discovery: Implement automated scanning of developer environments and CI/CD pipelines to detect unvetted third-party AI models or libraries that have bypassed formal intake processes.
Third-party components Include SBOM/MBOM and signature verification.
Policy & Standards: Secure coding standards; IaC policies; dependency policies; model packaging standards.
Secrets Hygiene: No secrets in code; vault-backed tokens; rotated credentials.
Threat Modeling: Required at design; include AI-specific abuse, data leakage, model theft.
Scanning: SAST/DAST/IAST/SCA; IaC scanning; model/package scanning; policy gates in CI/CD.
Provenance & Integrity: Signed builds; reproducible pipelines; attestation for artifacts/models.
Release Controls: Change approvals; environment promotion gates; SBOM/MBOM attached to releases.
Defect Management: Risk-based SLAs; prioritize exploitable vulns; maintain audit trails.
Penetration Testing Program: Define application-specific penetration requirements and scope.
IDE Security Plugins: Deploy security linting and real-time vulnerability feedback tools within developer IDEs to catch insecure coding patterns and hardcoded secrets at the point of creation.
Automated Scanning with CI/CD gates blocking critical issues; SBOM for all released components.
